pi_items.verified_value: Mumps dataset exists at this path but is NOT bundled with Epi Info 7
pi_items.source: GitHub Epi-Info/Epi-Info-Community-Edition releases documentation lists actual bundled projects: Salmonellosis, Community Health, E. coli, Hutt Youth Survey, Beef Prevention, Soho Cholera. No Mumps project mentioned. Web search of GitHub and CDC documentation found no reference to Mumps dataset.
Cannot verify table structure without access to actual Mumps.mdb file. Standard epidemiological datasets typically use table names like 'Oswego' (bundled example) or similar.

Epi Info 7 User Guide confirms Classic Analysis is a real module with statistical capabilities including FREQ, TABLES, LOGISTIC commands. CDC official documentation: https://www.cdc.gov/epiinfo/user-guide/classic-analysis/classicanalysisintro.html

pi_items.verified_value: All commands are real Epi Info 7 Classic Analysis commands
pi_items.source: CDC Epi Info documentation confirms: READ (data loading), FREQ (frequency analysis), TABLES (2x2 tables with odds ratios), LOGISTIC (logistic regression), ROUTEOUT (output redirection), WRITE (export data). See https://www.cdc.gov/epiinfo/user-guide/command-reference/analysis-commands-logistic.html

pi_items.metadata_value: Real Epi Info 7 bundled example dataset from CDC mumps outbreak investigation (from README.md)
pi_items.verified_value: NOT bundled with Epi Info 7. Actual bundled projects are: Salmonellosis, Community Health, E. coli, Hutt Youth Survey, Beef Prevention, Soho Cholera, Sample.prj (Oswego).
pi_items.source: GitHub Epi-Info/Epi-Info-Community-Edition releases (https://github.com/Epi-Info/Epi-Info-Community-Edition/releases) lists project additions/removals. No Mumps project ever mentioned. Web searches of CDC Epi Info documentation, GitHub repositories, and tutorials found no Mumps dataset. Oswego (foodborne outbreak) is the classic bundled epidemiological example.

pi_items.metadata_value: Using Classic Analysis TABLES for 2x2 contingency and LOGISTIC regression for vaccine effectiveness calculation
pi_items.verified_value: Standard epidemiological approach verified. VE = (1-OR)×100% formula is correct.
pi_items.source: CDC Epi Info documentation confirms TABLES generates odds ratios and LOGISTIC supports multivariate regression for vaccine effectiveness. Multiple published studies use this exact methodology for vaccine effectiveness analysis.

privileged_info_summary: The task uses Epi Info 7 Classic Analysis module (verified real software component) with real commands (READ, FREQ, TABLES, LOGISTIC, ROUTEOUT, WRITE). The vaccine effectiveness analysis methodology using 2x2 tables and logistic regression with VE=(1-OR)×100% formula is epidemiologically standard and verified. However, the Mumps dataset is NOT a real Epi Info 7 bundled example—Epi Info 7 ships with Salmonellosis, E. coli, Cholera, Oswego, and other projects, but no Mumps dataset exists in official releases or CDC documentation. The Mumps.mdb appears to be synthetically created for this task. The analysis framework, commands, and methodology are all grounded in real epidemiological practice and Epi Info 7 capabilities.
